2022-02-01.title: Angry U.S.-Russia Exchange at U.N. Punctuates Deepening Ukraine Rift
2022-02-01.abstract: With vitriolic accusations that echoed the Cold War era, the United States and Russia sparred in a bitter debate over the Ukraine crisis in a Security Council meeting watched by the world on Monday.

2022-02-01.title: A Year After Coup, Myanmar Is Mired in Conflict and Chaos
2022-02-01.abstract: Daily protests, once loud and colorful, have been replaced by an eerie quiet. To mark the anniversary of the military takeover, protest leaders have called for a “silent strike.”

2022-02-01.title: The U.S. orders families of its embassy staff in Belarus to leave the country.
2022-02-01.abstract: Washington cited “an increase in unusual and concerning Russian military activity near the border with Ukraine.”

2022-02-01.title: With Low-Tech, Trench Warfare, Ukraine’s Army Isn’t NATO Caliber
2022-02-01.abstract: The United States and its allies have trained and helped equip the Ukrainian army. But it has little resemblance to the kind of sophisticated, contemporary military that distinguishes NATO members.

2022-02-01.title: As the U.S. Pulls Back From the Mideast, China Leans In
2022-02-01.abstract: China is expanding its ties to Middle Eastern states with vast infrastructure investments and cooperation on technology and security.

2022-02-02.title: Tonga, struck by twin disasters, goes into lockdown over five virus cases.
2022-02-02.abstract: Among the infected are two workers who were helping to distribute aid shipments at the Tongan wharf after a volcanic eruption and tsunami tested positive.

2022-02-02.title: In Clash With U.S. Over Ukraine, Putin Has a Lifeline From China
2022-02-02.abstract: President Biden could find his plans to punish Russia undermined by Xi Jinping, a longtime ally of Mr. Putin. But China moves cautiously during crises.

2022-02-02.title: Shintaro Ishihara, Outspoken Nationalist Governor of Tokyo, Dies at 89
2022-02-02.abstract: An author as well as a politician, he was known for feuding with China over disputed islands and calling for Japan to assert itself against the United States.

2022-02-02.title: Conditions at a General Motors Plant in Mexico Spur a Labor Challenge
2022-02-02.abstract: Mexican salaries are low, partly because of unions that collude with employers. Workers at a G.M. factory are challenging that system, testing the labor reforms in the reworked North American trade deal.

2022-02-02.title: One Day in the ‘Parallel Universe’ of a London I.C.U.
2022-02-02.abstract: Britain’s government may have lifted coronavirus restrictions, but hospital workers say the return to a normal rhythm of work is still a long way off.

2022-02-03.title: On the eve of his meeting with Xi, Putin pledges to deepen ties with China.
2022-02-03.abstract: Russia’s president did not mention Ukraine, but said he and China’s leader would coordinate foreign policy “based on close and coinciding approaches.”

2022-02-03.title: ‘Live With the Virus’? For Australians, It’s Taken Some Getting Used To.
2022-02-03.abstract: Australia once stamped out every Covid outbreak. Now it’s done with all that. The policy U-turn, and the soaring case numbers, caught many off guard.

2022-02-03.title: Putin, Facing Sanction Threats, Has Been Saving for This Day
2022-02-03.abstract: Since paying the price for annexing Crimea in 2014, Russia has tried to make its economy sanctions-proof, hoarding currency to insulate the country.

2022-02-03.title: Read Biden’s statement on the ISIS leader’s death.
2022-02-03.abstract: Last night at my direction, U.S. military forces in northwest Syria successfully undertook a counterterrorism operation to protect the American people and our allies, and make the world a safer place. Thanks to the skill and bravery of our armed forces, we have taken off the battlefield Abu Ibrahim al-Hashimi al-Qurayshi — the leader of ISIS. All Americans have returned safely from the operation. I will deliver remarks to the American people later this morning. May God protect our troops.

2022-02-03.title: The Islamic State has shown that it can still pull off military operations.
2022-02-03.abstract: BEIRUT, Lebanon — One week after Islamic State fighters attacked a prison in northeastern Syria, where they have held out despite a heavy assault by a Kurdish-led militia backed by the United States, the terrorist organization published its version of what had gone down.

2022-02-04.title: Tracking Coronavirus in China: Latest Case Count
2022-02-04.abstract: See the latest charts of coronavirus cases and deaths in China.

2022-02-04.title: Why I’ve Learned to Loathe Australia’s Trivial Politics
2022-02-04.abstract: This week has shown just how ill-equipped the current Australian political ecosystem is when facing catastrophe.

2022-02-04.title: New Zealand Inquiry Finds Hundreds of Reports of Abuse by Priests
2022-02-04.abstract: The complaints, going back seven decades, attest to the pervasiveness of sexual and other abuse within the Catholic Church and are part of a worldwide reckoning.

2022-02-04.title: A Black Iraqi’s Sudden Career in TV News: ‘They Wanted to See All Colors’
2022-02-04.abstract: Randa Abd Al-Aziz was joking with friends in a Baghdad cafe when she got a surprise job offer: broadcasting the news in Iraq, whose Black citizens have little media presence and even less power.

2022-02-04.title: On Ukraine, Many Russians Have No Doubt: It’s the Fault of the U.S.
2022-02-04.abstract: The idea that the U.S. and NATO are fomenting the crisis between Russia and Ukraine is hammered home daily by the Kremlin’s propaganda machine.

2022-02-05.title: ‘Guernica’ Antiwar Tapestry Is Rehung at U.N.
2022-02-05.abstract: The canvas replica of Picasso’s painting, symbolizing war’s horrors, had been a photogenic fixture at the United Nations for decades before its owner, the Rockefeller family, removed it last year.

2022-02-05.title: Africa Cup of Nations: Soccer Tournament Offers Joy Amid Coups and Covid
2022-02-05.abstract: Many countries competing in the Africa Cup of Nations are enduring security, economic and political crises, but the tournament has brought delight and solidarity.

2022-02-05.title: Facing Tough Election, Orban Turns to Putin for Support
2022-02-05.abstract: The Hungarian leader made his name by defying Moscow. But he has increasingly turned toward Russia in an effort to secure the natural gas he needs to keep energy prices low and voters happy.

2022-02-05.title: Leader’s Death Is Another Blow for ISIS, but It’s Hardly the End
2022-02-05.abstract: The Islamic State is a shadow of its former self. But it’s likely to keep adapting and enduring, even after the U.S. raid that killed its top commander.

2022-02-05.title: A Side-Effect of China’s Strict Virus Policy: Abandoned Fruit
2022-02-05.abstract: The closure of Chinese land borders and the tightened screening of goods have driven Southeast Asian fruit farmers into debt. Many have had to abandon their harvest.

2022-02-06.title: Truck Convoy Protests Descend on Major Canadian Cities
2022-02-06.abstract: Demonstrations that began with truckers critical of vaccine mandates continued for a second straight weekend, as thousands across Ottawa, Toronto and Quebec City protested for a number of political causes.

2022-02-06.title: Queen Elizabeth Paves the Way for Camilla to One Day Be Called Queen
2022-02-06.abstract: On the eve of the 70th anniversary of the start of her reign, Elizabeth said she wanted the Duchess of Cornwall to hold the title of Queen Consort once Prince Charles is king.

2022-02-06.title: Lata Mangeshkar, Bollywood’s Most Beloved Voice, Dies at 92
2022-02-06.abstract: Ms. Mangeshkar, a revered figure in India, recorded songs for countless films — not appearing onscreen herself, but providing characters’ singing voices.

2022-02-06.title: Queen Elizabeth, Anchor in a Storm-Tossed Britain, Marks 70-Year Reign
2022-02-06.abstract: The milestone puts the queen, 95, in rarefied company: Only three other monarchs are documented to have reigned for more than 70 years.

2022-02-06.title: Germany’s ‘Invisible’ Chancellor Heads to Washington Amid Fierce Criticism
2022-02-06.abstract: Olaf Scholz will try to repair Germany’s credibility in the Ukraine crisis when he meets President Biden on Monday. Next on his agenda: Kyiv and Moscow.

2022-02-07.title: Ottawa Mayor Declares State of Emergency Amid Antigovernment Protests
2022-02-07.abstract: A 10-day demonstration by truckers and other protesters has ensnarled Canada’s capital and led officials to warn that things could turn violent.

2022-02-07.title: ‘Democracy Is Life’: The Grass-Roots Movement Taking On Sudan’s Generals
2022-02-07.abstract: Hundreds of loosely connected “resistance committees” are organizing nonviolent protests, tracking the injured and dead and demanding a government led by civilians.

2022-02-07.title: Turkey’s Doctors Are Leaving, the Latest Casualty of Spiraling Inflation
2022-02-07.abstract: Doctors, worn down by grueling hours and violence, are emigrating in rising numbers, undermining one of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s signature achievements.

2022-02-07.title: Two years after his death, the Chinese doctor who warned of the virus is remembered.
2022-02-07.abstract: Social media users flocked to an online profile for Dr. Li Wenliang, who provided an early warning about the coronavirus in 2019.

2022-02-07.abstract: Taiwan knows what it’s like to have an overbearing neighbor. China wonders how forcefully Western powers might react to a Russian invasion.

2022-02-08.title: As Protest Paralyzes Canada’s Capital, Far-Right Activists Abroad Embrace It
2022-02-08.abstract: It may be only a few hundred demonstrators occupying Ottawa’s streets to protest government overreach, but their message has aroused passions around the world.

2022-02-08.title: Putin Warns the West and Ukraine, but Keeps His Intentions a Mystery
2022-02-08.abstract: President Vladimir Putin said diplomatic ideas raised in a meeting by French President Emmanuel Macron were worth pursuing, but did not rule out a Russian invasion of Ukraine.

2022-02-08.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron of France traveled to Moscow to meet with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia, amid mounting Western fears that Russia could attack neighboring Ukraine in the coming weeks.

2022-02-08.title: As Officials Look Away, Hate Speech in India Nears Dangerous Levels
2022-02-08.abstract: Activists and analysts say calls for anti-Muslim violence — even genocide — are moving from the fringes to the mainstream, while political leaders keep silent.

2022-02-08.abstract: The location is meant to protect its most powerful weapons from pre-emptive strikes, allowing the military to expand an already rapidly growing arsenal.

2022-02-09.title: Photographer’s Death Casts Harsh Light on the Cold Streets of Paris
2022-02-09.abstract: René Robert, known for his pictures of flamenco, died of hypothermia after falling on a busy road, seemingly unassisted. The death shocked many, but echoed the indifference homeless people face every day.

2022-02-09.abstract: The South Korean curling team helped revive a painful discussion about the mistreatment of athletes in their country, leading the way for others to come forward.

2022-02-09.abstract: With Russian forces menacing from three sides, Ukrainians are stoically keeping to their daily routines, but reminders of the looming threat are getting hard to avoid.

2022-02-09.title: New York is dropping its indoor mask mandate, joining other blue states in easing Covid rules.
2022-02-09.abstract: Gov. Kathy Hochul dropped New York’s stringent indoor mask mandate on Wednesday, ending a requirement that businesses ask customers for proof of full vaccination or require mask wearing at all times, and marking a turning point in the state’s coronavirus response, according to three people briefed on her decision.

2022-02-09.title: In South Korea and other Asian countries, a surge follows Lunar New Year.
2022-02-09.abstract: A spike in cases across Asia has followed this month’s holiday.

2022-02-10.abstract: The Ottawa protesters are giddy with their sense of collective purpose. But local residents see the demonstration as an unruly, disrespectful and even dangerous occupation.

2022-02-10.title: Top British officials lead diplomatic efforts on Thursday.

2022-02-10.title: European leaders say they seek peace, and warn Russia of retaliation for any aggression against Ukraine.
2022-02-10.abstract: After days of intense diplomacy over the Ukraine crisis, the leaders of France, Germany and Poland said this week that their overriding goal was the preservation of peace in Europe, but warned Russia of dire consequences if it launched further incursions into Ukraine.

2022-02-10.abstract: While many voters say they are concerned about the economy, the prime minister’s party has placed a focus on religion, with often polarizing effects.

2022-02-10.abstract: Covid-era travel restrictions have mostly prevented expatriate aid workers from flying into Pacific island nations after natural disasters. The results are mixed.

2022-02-11.abstract: Peter Sloly, Ottawa’s police chief, said the protesters were increasingly using sophisticated tactics to target law-enforcement operations, such as flooding emergency lines with fake 911 calls, many originating from the United States.

2022-02-11.title: The Chinese men’s hockey team has an international cast.

2022-02-11.abstract: Antony Blinken’s Asia-Pacific trip during the crisis with Russia and Ukraine signals that the U.S. is committing to the world’s largest region — and to competition with China.

2022-02-12.abstract: Despite a mass of protesters demanding an end to vaccine mandates and coronavirus restrictions in Canada, polls have shown that a majority of Canadians support the pandemic health measures.

2022-02-12.abstract: The French leader recounted his face-off with Vladimir Putin and dismissed Washington’s exchange of letters with Moscow, gambling that his diplomacy could pay off before April elections.

2022-02-13.abstract: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken and his counterparts from South Korea and Japan denounced the North’s recent missile tests and called for a resumption of talks.

2022-02-13.abstract: “It is a cruel act and a betrayal,” one shopkeeper said of the White House’s move, which could effectively bankrupt the Afghan central bank.

2022-02-14.abstract: Amid progress at a border bridge, Ottawa remained frozen by an antigovernment protest. There were signs of a slight thaw, with truckers said to be weighing pulling out of part of the capital.

2022-02-14.abstract: Ukrainian forces waged intense battles to hold major cities, including the capital, Kyiv.

2022-02-15.abstract: “We cannot and will not allow illegal and dangerous activities to continue,” Canada’s prime minister said in a speech to the nation.

2022-02-15.abstract: Juan Orlando Hernández, who stepped down as president last month, was recently accused in a drug-trafficking case in federal court in New York.

Putin of Russia in Moscow on Tuesday, becoming the latest Western leader to embark on shuttle diplomacy to defuse the crisis in Eastern Europe.

2022-02-17.abstract: The pullout came after a falling out between France and Mali, in West Africa, ending a military effort that failed to drive out violent extremists.

2022-02-17.abstract: With Omicron spreading quickly, the government says it will focus on the most vulnerable, while asking others to look after themselves at home.

2022-02-17.abstract: “We’ve said for some time that the Russians might do something like this in order to justify a military conflict,” said the U.S. defense secretary, Lloyd J. Austin III. “So we’ll be watching this very closely.”

2022-02-17.abstract: The happy complacency of post-Cold War peace is being shattered by Russia’s threats, demands and massive military buildup around Ukraine.

2022-02-18.abstract: After weeks of truckers and others blocking border crossings and streets in the capital city, the demonstrations appeared to be nearing an end.

2022-02-18.abstract: A fire broke out Wednesday morning in the cargo hold of the ship, which departed from Emden, Germany, on Feb. 10 and was headed to Davisville, R.I.

2022-02-18.abstract: In recent years, Japan, China and South Korea have embraced the sport, investing resources, creating celebrities and changing the competitive landscape.

2022-02-18.abstract: As fears of a Russian invasion of Ukraine mount, President Biden is set to speak with global allies on Friday afternoon about Moscow’s buildup of military troops, in a continued effort to deter any hostile advances on its neighbor.

2022-02-20.title: A Covid pill from Merck showed more promise in reducing the risk of hospitalization in a recent study.
2022-02-20.abstract: The antiviral pill molnupiravir reduced the risk of Covid-19 hospitalization by 65 percent in a new study by Indian researchers that offered stronger results than previous research about the drug’s effectiveness.

2022-02-20.title: Some believe China was the birthplace of skiing.

2022-02-20.abstract: A defeat here, one of the few states where the once-dominant party still retains power, would be a major blow.

2022-02-20.abstract: For weeks, as Russia sent more and more troops to Ukraine’s borders, the Biden administration has predicted that President Vladimir V. Putin would orchestrate some sort of pretext for an invasion, probably in Ukraine’s east, where a conflict with Moscow-backed rebels has been underway for years.

2022-02-20.title: With Olympics Closing Ceremony, China Celebrates a Joyless Triumph
2022-02-20.abstract: The Games ended without disaster but overshadowed by a doping scandal, rising tensions in Europe and anxiety over the future of the sporting movement.

2022-02-21.title: If War Flames in Ukraine, These 2 Little Enclaves May Be the Spark
2022-02-21.abstract: Russian-backed separatists in the Luhansk and Donetsk regions once got just passing attention. They loom much larger now as many fear what may come next in Ukraine.

2022-02-21.title: Putin’s Security Council meets as the Kremlin warns that ‘tensions are rising.’
2022-02-21.abstract: The United States says Russia stands poised to attack Ukraine amid a frantic diplomatic push.

2022-02-21.title: At a pro-Ukraine rally, priests pray for the dead and diplomats hope for peace.

2022-02-21.abstract: A U.S. ambassador cited “credible information” that Russian forces have compiled a list of Ukrainians to be targeted after an invasion.

2022-02-22.abstract: The moves by the Russian leader were his most blatant yet in a confrontation with the West that threatens to escalate into the biggest military action in Europe since World War II.

2022-02-22.abstract: In a speech, President Vladimir V. Putin bent Ukraine’s complex history into his own version that served as a justification for his cleaving off more of its territory.

2022-02-22.title: U.S. diplomatic staff relocate from Ukraine to Poland.
2022-02-22.abstract: The U.S. State Department announced Monday night that diplomatic employees in Lviv, in western Ukraine, would spend the night in Poland.

2022-02-22.title: Colombia Decriminalizes Abortion, Bolstering Trend Across Region
2022-02-22.abstract: The court’s decision follows years of organizing by women across Latin America for greater protections and more rights, and significant shifts in the region’s legal landscape.

2022-02-22.title: The conflict’s economic consequences are likely to threaten global growth.
2022-02-22.abstract: The looming danger of a war on the European Union’s border means yet another uncertainty for a global economy that has already been hurt by the pandemic, supply chain chokeholds and inflation.

2022-02-23.title: U.N. leader deepens criticism of Russia over Ukraine crisis.
2022-02-23.abstract: António Guterres said Ukraine’s sovereign territory had been violated, and he rejected Vladimir Putin’s description of Russian troops as peacekeepers.

2022-02-23.title: New satellite images show military deployments continuing along the Ukrainian border.
2022-02-23.abstract: One week after President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia announced a partial withdrawal of troops from the border with Ukraine, newly released satellite images show a different reality. In addition to redeployments in the Belgorod-Valuyki area in western Russia, where there has been a lot of military activity over recent days, new military activities continue to emerge in Belarus.

2022-02-23.title: Ireland ends mask mandates in most places and social distancing in schools.
2022-02-23.abstract: Ireland will end social distancing in schools and lift its mask requirement “in all settings where currently regulated,” officials announced on Tuesday. Public health officials will still recommend the use of masks on public transportation and in health care settings.

2022-02-23.title: Global leaders send a message with coordinated penalties.
2022-02-23.abstract: WASHINGTON — Russia faced mounting pressure and economic sanctions over the Ukraine crisis on Wednesday as the United States and allies coordinated punishments and denounced the beginning of an “invasion of Ukraine.”

2022-02-23.title: New Zealand Protest, an Echo of Canada’s, Digs In and Turns Ugly
2022-02-23.abstract: An occupation in the capital demonstrates the dangerous influence that American disinformation is having on otherwise stable democracies.

2022-02-24.title: Putin Announces Start to ‘Military Operation’ Against Ukraine
2022-02-24.abstract: “The invasion has begun,” Ukraine’s Interior Ministry said. Putin says he seeks “demilitarization” of Ukraine but has no plans to occupy it.

2022-02-24.title: Putin announces a ‘military operation’ in Ukraine as the U.N. Security Council pleads with him to pull back.
2022-02-24.abstract: MOSCOW — President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia declared the start of a “special military operation” in Ukraine, pledging he would seek to demilitarize but not occupy the country, addressing the nation in a televised speech broadcast just before 6 a.m. local time.

2022-02-24.title: Video of Large Explosions Near Kharkiv, Ukraine
2022-02-24.abstract: Minutes after President Vladimir Putin announced a military operation in eastern Ukraine, explosions were visible near the city of Kharkiv.

2022-02-24.title: Biden vows to ‘hold Russia accountable’ and Congress calls for tougher sanctions.
2022-02-24.abstract: The Russian invasion of Ukraine set off a rare moment of bipartisanship, as lawmakers furiously denounced the attack and called on Mr. Biden to swiftly enact new, debilitating sanctions on Russia.

2022-02-24.title: Japan, hesitant to impose sanctions on Russia in 2014, embraces them now. One reason: China.
2022-02-24.abstract: Japan was previously reluctant to impose sanctions.

2022-02-25.title: The White House is mulling a pandemic strategy for ‘the next normal.’
2022-02-25.abstract: President Biden’s coronavirus response team is evaluating a 136-page blueprint by a team of outside experts who have spent much of this week meeting behind closed doors with White House officials.

2022-02-25.title: Russian forces are meeting more resistance near Kyiv and Kharkiv than farther south, analysts say.
2022-02-25.abstract: Independent security analysts said Russian forces in the east and the south of Ukraine were maneuvering with less resistance than those forces that were farther north and approaching the cities of Kyiv and Kharkiv, where the attacks were less sophisticated and the Ukrainian military had succeeded in engaging Moscow’s army.

2022-02-25.title: Zelensky says Russian saboteurs are in Kyiv and he is Moscow’s prime target.
2022-02-25.abstract: Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, said that Russian saboteurs had entered Kyiv, the capital, and that he was “target No. 1” for Russian forces, followed by his family.

2022-02-25.title: Russia Batters Ukraine With Artillery Strikes as West Condemns Invasion
2022-02-25.abstract: Explosions were reported over Kyiv, the capital. Several countries announced severe new sanctions, as Vladimir Putin remained resolute about his decision to invade.

2022-02-25.title: Another casualty of Russia’s invasion: Ukraine’s ability to contain the coronavirus.
2022-02-25.abstract: The fighting in Ukraine’s east is forcing a hurried mass migration that leaves little room for pandemic considerations.

2022-02-26.title: Ukraine Girds for Kyiv Attack Amid Signs of Slowing Russian Advance
2022-02-26.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky warned in a video address that Russian “sabotage groups” had entered Kyiv with the aim of decapitating his government by “destroying the head of state.”

2022-02-26.title: Canada approves a second vaccine made without mRNA technology.
2022-02-26.abstract: The clearance given to a plant-based offering developed by Medicago of Quebec City only covers people ages 18 to 64.

2022-02-26.title: Russia vetoes a U.N. Security Council resolution calling on it to withdraw from Ukraine.
2022-02-26.abstract: Eleven countries voted in favor of the U.S.-backed measure, and three abstained.

2022-02-26.title: Landmarks in New York and elsewhere use lights to show support for Ukraine.
2022-02-26.abstract: The landmarks include the World Trade Center, the Eiffel Tower and more.

2022-02-26.title: On both sides of the Korean Peninsula, eyes are on Washington’s response to Russia.
2022-02-26.abstract: North Korea might decide it’s time to resume weapons tests, to gain more diplomatic leverage with Washington.

2022-02-27.title: Ukrainians escape to neighboring Poland.
2022-02-27.abstract: More than 150,000 refugees have fled Ukraine this week, according to the United Nations, with many heading toward Polish border crossings.

2022-02-27.title: North Korea Launches a Ballistic Missile, South Korea Says
2022-02-27.abstract: Flight data suggested that the missile launched Sunday was less powerful than the last one the North tested, four weeks ago.

2022-02-27.title: ‘I Just Want to Live in Our Country’: Civilians Join the Fight in Kyiv
2022-02-27.abstract: Julia, a teacher and Ukrainian volunteer, wept as she waited to be deployed to fight Russian troops around Kyiv on Saturday.

2022-02-27.title: South Korea records its deadliest day of the pandemic.
2022-02-27.abstract: A grim milestone is reached a few days after the nation topped 170,000 new cases in a single day.

2022-02-27.title: Brazil, Land of the Thong, Embraces Its Heavier Self
2022-02-27.abstract: A country known for beach bodies is confronting soaring obesity rates with new laws that enshrine protections for people who are overweight.

2022-02-28.title: The mask mandate in the Capitol is being lifted in time for the State of the Union.
2022-02-28.abstract: This year’s address from the president, unlike last year’s, is open to all members of Congress.

2022-02-28.title: Kyrie Irving still won’t be able to play in Brooklyn if the mayor lifts some Covid restrictions next week.
2022-02-28.abstract: Barclays Center would remain under a private-sector vaccine mandate that took effect in December.

2022-02-28.title: Here’s the latest news from Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
2022-02-28.abstract: As Ukraine’s second largest city reeled under a barrage of Russian rockets that left dozens of people dead, officials said, a Ukrainian delegation met counterparts from Russia on Monday in Belarus for talks that held little hope for an immediate end to the fighting that has sparked a nationwide resistance in Ukraine and left Russia’s economy increasingly isolated from the world.

2022-02-28.title: Singapore’s Latest Ruling on Gay Sex Is ‘Cold Comfort,’ Activists Say
2022-02-28.abstract: Plaintiffs had hoped the Court of Appeal would overturn the colonial-era law. Instead, the top court said it was not “an architect of social policy” and that any change was up to Parliament.

2022-02-28.title: Threats Emerge in Germany as Far Right and Pandemic Protesters Merge
2022-02-28.abstract: In the former Communist East, security officials worry that a long-running movement against Covid restrictions is becoming an incubator of political violence.
